trim_reasons: REB is confirmed slump-persistent and iron_floor is absent here; with a VOLATILE tag and overall only 75% at this tier (borderline qualification), a single low-rebound game is well within Jokic's wide distribution. The typical_miss of only 2.0 pts below the line means even a modest off-rebounding night produces a loss.

AST props on VOLATILE players against tough defenses have historically underperformed in this system; Murray's raw_avg_AST of 6.8 gives some cushion, but the opp_defense is rated tough and audit history confirms VOLATILE AST T4 picks in playoff road games are the floor of what the system should be picking. At minimum confidence (70%) with no iron_floor, a single suppressed playmaking night ends the bet.

BLOWOUT_RISK with spread=-8.5 means if CLE goes up big early, Mitchell's counting minutes in Q4 are at risk; even after the blowout penalty was applied, a large home-favorite spread creates genuine Q4 rest exposure for a star on a hot team. The min_floor of 30.3 is reasonable but not ironclad in a 15+ point blowout scenario.

BLOWOUT_RISK combined with a VOLATILE AST tag is the exact scenario audit history identifies as the single greatest failure point for AST props — blowout game-scripts collapse playmaking opportunities for the winning team's stars who get benched in Q4, and Mitchell's raw_avg_AST of only 5.0 provides minimal buffer over the T4 line. Historical audit explicitly flags that blowout environments suppress AST for winning-side playmakers in ways the iron_floor (absent here) cannot protect against.

NYK's home defensive identity in a playoff game suppressed ATL's offensive flow in the prior audit miss (Johnson AST T4, 3 assists in 39 minutes vs NYK); the same defensive structure applies here. FG_COLD:-12% and a tough opp_defense rating means Johnson's 70% minimum-confidence PTS floor is genuinely fragile against a motivated home defense.

The bounce_back post_miss_hr for Johnson AST is only 58% with a negative lift (0.79) — the worst bounce-back profile on the entire slate. If Johnson missed his AST tier last game, the expected hit rate next game is materially lower, and audit history has a recent direct miss on Johnson AST T4 vs NYK in exactly this matchup context.

min_floor of 19.8 is below 24 on a PTS pick — a single low-minutes game (foul trouble, blowout bench rest) is well within normal variance and produces a miss at T10. KAT's STRONG CAUTION PTS playoff adjustment was already applied, but the minutes fragility structurally undermines the floor in ways the hit rate alone does not capture.

min_floor of 19.8 combined with VOLATILE tag and a REB prop where the ceiling rule was borderline-qualified (75% = 7.5/10) means this pick sits at the intersection of minutes fragility and distributional width — any foul trouble or tactical adjustment by ATL to limit KAT's post touches produces a miss.

CLE's blowout spread (-8.5) creates a game-script risk for TOR's secondary playmakers: if TOR falls behind early, Barnes's offensive role compresses as the team chases points rather than running structured offense that generates assists. At T4 with raw_avg_AST of 7.6, the line is beatable in most games, but the losing-side blowout game-script specifically collapses structured playmaking.

Ingram carries a VOLATILE tag with a post_miss_hr of only 72% and a negative bounce-back lift (0.93), meaning he does not reliably rebound after a miss — the weakest PTS bounce-back profile among TOR players. Combined with the blowout game-script risk as the underdog and FG_MARGIN_THIN triggering a mandatory step-down, this is the most structurally fragile TOR PTS pick on the slate.

BLOWOUT_RISK with spread=-8.5 directly threatens Harden's minutes in Q4; if CLE goes up by 15+ in the third quarter, Harden is a prime candidate for early benching given his age and load management profile, and the pick has no iron_floor protection at T10 to absorb a shortened night.

BLOWOUT_RISK + VOLATILE AST tag on the primary playmaker of the favored team is the highest-risk AST combination in the system — audit history explicitly identifies this as the scenario where winning-side playmakers lose Q4 minutes and AST opportunities collapse. Harden's post_miss_hr for AST is only 67% with a negative lift (0.86), and there is no iron_floor. This is structurally identical to the blowout AST failure pattern documented in recent audit losses.

min_floor of 22.0 is below 24, meaning a single early-blowout Q4 benching (BLOWOUT_RISK fires at -8.5) is structurally sufficient to miss the 6-REB line without any statistical anomaly. The H2H qualifying rate was only 50% before falling back to overall, and the quant tier is 6 — this pick is sitting at exactly its qualified floor.

The bounce_back post_miss_hr for Mobley AST is only 60% with a strongly negative lift (0.70) — the worst bounce-back profile on the entire slate for AST. BLOWOUT_RISK further compresses Mobley's second-half playmaking touches if CLE rests starters, and a C position in a blowout situation is the first role to see reduced late-game involvement.

Randle's raw_avg_REB of only 6.5 sits barely above the T6 line, and with a VOLATILE tag his distribution is wide enough that the typical_miss of 1.0 below the line means he misses by a single rebound in roughly 25% of games. The step-down from T8 (H2H 1/5 vs DEN) signals genuine DEN-specific suppression of his rebounding that the overall hit rate may not fully capture.

Edwards is QUES with a knee injury (-5% shooting penalty applied) and carries FG_COLD:-19% which triggered a mandatory step-down cascade; the post_miss_hr is only 55% with a deeply negative lift (0.66), the worst PTS bounce-back on the entire slate. A VOLATILE tag on a player with an active soft-tissue injury who is already in a shooting slump represents genuine floor fragility even at the stepped-down T15 tier.

Edwards has an active knee injury with a -5% shooting penalty on 3PM props and a FG_COLD:-19% signal; trend=down triggered a mandatory step to T1, and the overall at T1 is only 75% — not an iron_floor or 9/10 rate. A cold-shooting injured guard on the road in a playoff game is structurally one of the most volatile 3PM scenarios possible.
